title: Ecbolium carthaginense
intro: Ecbolium carthaginense, also known as Carthaginian bugloss or blue bugloss, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as arthritis and improving overall cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Ecbolium carthaginense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Ecbolium carthaginense is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Ecbolium carthaginense is classified within the family Acanthaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales of flowering plants. Ecbolium carthaginense further belongs to the genus Ecbolium. The family Acanthaceae consists of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or thorny flowers. The exact classification of Ecbolium carthaginense can be found in various botanical resources.
